original_hypothesis.title: Whitelisted Contract Gas Limit DoS via Unlimited Delegates
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: `ERC20MultiVotes` allows governance to whitelist contracts to bypass `maxDelegates` via `setContractExceedMaxDelegates`. If a whitelisted contract accumulates a massive number of delegates, any attempt to transfer or burn its tokens will trigger `_decrementVotesUntilFree()`, which loops over all delegates. This loop will exceed the block gas limit, permanently locking the contract's tokens.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While this requires governance whitelisting, it represents a severe footgun for protocol integrations (e.g., yield aggregators or smart wallets) that could permanently lock user funds. Validation requires testing the gas cost of `_decrementVotesUntilFree` with hundreds of delegates.
deployment_assumptions: Governance whitelists a contract to exceed max delegates
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: GuildToken.setContractExceedMaxDelegates and CreditToken.setContractExceedMaxDelegates functions exist to enable this.
preconditions: A contract is whitelisted to exceed maxDelegates
The contract delegates to a large number of addresses

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: O(N) loop over delegates during token transfer
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker delegates to thousands of addresses on behalf of the contract (if the contract allows arbitrary delegation)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Griefing attack to lock funds in a competitor's protocol integration.

economic_questions: How many delegates are required to hit the block gas limit?
Can the contract undelegate in smaller batches to recover?
validation_requirements: Measure gas cost per delegate in _decrementVotesUntilFree.

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users of the whitelisted integration contract
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% loss of mobility for deposited tokens

validation_result.impact_summary: Whitelisted contracts that accumulate many delegates will be permanently unable to transfer or burn tokens due to block gas limit exhaustion.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `ERC20MultiVotes` contract includes a `maxDelegates` limit specifically to prevent gas exhaustion during token transfers, as noted in the security comments. However, it also provides a governance-controlled whitelist (`canContractExceedMaxDelegates`) to bypass this limit. If a whitelisted contract (such as a yield aggregator or smart wallet) accumulates a large number of delegates, any token transfer or burn that requires freeing votes will trigger `_decrementVotesUntilFree`. This function calls `_delegates[user].values()`, which copies the entire `EnumerableSet` of delegates into memory (an O(N) operation). It then iterates over the array, performing expensive storage operations (`remove`, `_writeCheckpoint`) for each delegate until enough votes are freed. If the number of delegates is sufficiently large, the memory allocation and loop execution will exceed the block gas limit, causing the transaction to revert and permanently locking the contract's tokens unless it has a separate mechanism to manually `undelegate` in small batches.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof of signal accurately points to `_decrementVotesUntilFree`, where `_delegates[user].values()` is called and iterated over. The `_incrementDelegation` function explicitly allows whitelisted contracts to bypass `maxDelegates`, enabling the unbounded growth of the `_delegates` set.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When `userFreeVotes < votes`, the function copies the entire `_delegates` set to memory using `.values()`. For a whitelisted contract with thousands of delegates, this memory allocation alone can consume excessive gas. The subsequent loop performs multiple storage writes per iteration, guaranteeing a block gas limit exhaustion.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
bool newDelegate = _delegates[delegator].add(delegatee); // idempotent add
        require(
            !newDelegate ||
                delegateCount(delegator) <= maxDelegates ||
                canContractExceedMaxDelegates[delegator],
            "ERC20MultiVotes: delegation error"
        );
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This check allows contracts with `canContractExceedMaxDelegates` set to true to bypass the `maxDelegates` limit, enabling the unbounded growth of the `_delegates` set that causes the DoS in `_decrementVotesUntilFree`.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When a whitelisted contract calls `transfer` and its `freeVotes` are less than the transfer `amount`, `_decrementVotesUntilFree` is invoked. The function calls `_delegates[user].values()`, which iterates over the entire unbounded `EnumerableSet` to copy it to memory. It then loops over the array, modifying storage for each delegate. The unbounded size of the set causes the transaction to run out of gas.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Governance whitelists a contract to exceed max delegates
config_validation.deployment_evidence: The provided deployment scripts (`DeployProposal.s.sol`) do not explicitly whitelist any contract using `setContractExceedMaxDelegates`. However, the function exists and is intended for this exact use case.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: An attacker identifies a protocol integration (e.g., a smart wallet or yield aggregator) that has been whitelisted by governance to exceed `maxDelegates`. If the integration allows users to specify delegation targets, the attacker delegates to thousands of unique addresses on behalf of the integration contract. When any user attempts to withdraw funds from the integration contract, the underlying `transfer` call triggers `_decrementVotesUntilFree`, which reverts due to block gas limit exhaustion, permanently locking all funds in the integration contract.
validation_result.impact_assessment: High impact for the affected whitelisted contract, as all its tokens become permanently locked. The severity depends on whether governance whitelists vulnerable integration contracts.
recommended_mitigations: Instead of copying the entire `_delegates` set to memory using `.values()`, iterate over the set using `.at(index)` and only read the necessary elements.
Limit the number of delegates that can be freed in a single transaction, or require whitelisted contracts to manually manage their free balance via `undelegate` before transferring.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy `CreditToken`. 2. Call `setContractExceedMaxDelegates` for a test contract. 3. Have the test contract mint/receive tokens and delegate to 2000 unique addresses. 4. Attempt to transfer tokens from the test contract. 5. Observe the transaction reverting with an Out of Gas error.
uncertainties: Whether governance will actually whitelist contracts that allow arbitrary user-controlled delegation.
additional_testing_needed: Benchmark the exact gas cost of `_decrementVotesUntilFree` per delegate to determine the threshold number of delegates required to hit the block gas limit.
